notes: RFC 8391 (informational) and NIST SP 800-208 (normative) specify XMSS-SHA2_10_256 but do not publish KAT vectors. This test uses the _set_kat_seed hook (softhsmv3 Rust WASM) to inject a known 96-byte seed (SK_SEED||SK_PRF||PUB_SEED = all zeros) and prove that keygen is deterministic — the derived public key must match expected_pub_key_hex on every run. Height h=10 (2^10=1024 signatures), n=32, w=16. Signature length: 2500 bytes (RFC 8391 §4 formula: 4+4+67×32+10×32 = 2500).
kat_seed_structure: SK_SEED(32 bytes, zeros) || SK_PRF(32 bytes, zeros) || PUB_SEED(32 bytes, zeros)
